text.title: Stakeholder Engagement (Advanced) Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Polite-tone bias: agents up-rate engagement on courteous emails.
antipattern.fix: Require explicit positive evidence ('agreed to sponsor', 'publicly endorsed') before upgrading.

antipattern.symptom: Agent proposes moving Resistant directly to Leading in one step.
antipattern.root-cause: Skipped levels produce shallow engagement that collapses.
antipattern.fix: Force one-level moves; Resistant → Neutral hop must be earned with concrete commitment.

antipattern.root-cause: LLM personalisation tokens substituted into a generic template fool nobody.
antipattern.fix: Agent surfaces context bullets; the human writes the email body.

antipattern.symptom: Engagement level classification shared with the stakeholder themselves.
antipattern.fix: Treat levels as internal; never let an agent share classification with the stakeholder.
